chore: update GitHub Actions for Node.js 24 compatibility

- Add FORCE_JAVASCRIPT_ACTIONS_TO_NODE24 environment variable
- Update Node.js version in build job from 18 to 22
- Prepare for Node.js 20 deprecation (June 2nd, 2026)
- Actions already support Node.js 24:
  * actions/checkout@v4
  * actions/upload-artifact@v4
  * actions/download-artifact@v4
  * google-github-actions/auth@v2
  * google-github-actions/setup-gcloud@v2
  * hashicorp/setup-terraform@v3

commit.message:
feat: implement OpenViking-inspired capabilities (hotness score, used() feedback, L0/L1/L2, fast/quality paths, memory taxonomy, tool stats)

Borrowed 5 high-value capabilities from volcengine/OpenViking, maximally
leveraging openJiuwen framework built-ins (AgenticRetriever for quality path,
rrf_fusion framework already available).

New capabilities:
- Hotness Score: sigmoid(log1p(active_count)) × exp(-λ×age_days) blended
  into RRF fusion at alpha=0.2 (context_agent/core/memory/hotness.py)
- used() feedback API: POST /context/used increments active_count on
  confirmed context items, driving Hotness Score
- MemoryCategory 6-taxonomy: profile/preferences/entities/events/cases/patterns
  with AggregationRequest.category_filter support
- ContextLevel L0/L1/L2: ABSTRACT/OVERVIEW/DETAIL levels with
  AggregationRequest.max_level progressive expansion
- fast/quality dual-path: mode='quality' activates AgenticRetriever
  (openJiuwen-native) for complex tasks; mode='fast' (default) uses
  HybridRetriever for low-latency simple queries
- Tool Performance Memory: ToolContextGovernor.record_tool_result() +
  POST /tools/result endpoint; unreliable tools rank lower in select_tools()

Files changed:
- context_agent/models/context.py: +ContextLevel, +MemoryCategory enums,
  +ContextItem.{level,category,active_count,updated_at} fields
- context_agent/core/memory/hotness.py: new hotness pure function module
- context_agent/core/retrieval/search_coordinator.py: hotness-blended RRF
- context_agent/core/memory/working_memory.py: +mark_used() method
- context_agent/core/memory/tiered_router.py: +record_usage() method
- context_agent/core/retrieval/tool_governor.py: +record_tool_result(), +_sort_by_success_rate()
- context_agent/adapters/ltm_adapter.py: +agentic_search() with AgenticRetriever fallback
- context_agent/orchestration/context_aggregator.py: +mode/max_level/category_filter params
- context_agent/api/schemas.py: +ContextUsedRequest/Response, +ToolResultRequest/Response
- context_agent/api/http_handler.py: +POST /context/used, +POST /tools/result
- context_agent/api/router.py: +mark_used(), +record_tool_result() facade methods
- tests/unit/test_openviking_capabilities.py: 38 new unit tests (all passing)
- docs/architecture-design.md: +Appendix D with capability details
- docs/agent-integration-guide.md: +Section 9 with usage guide

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: use dynamic year in footer copyright

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: haya-rs
repository.description: A re-created version of the gotrue/supabase-auth service.

commit.message:
feat: overhaul to Supabase Auth (GoTrue) compatible drop-in replacement

- Replace Cargo.toml with new dependencies (dotenvy, jsonwebtoken, argon2, thiserror, tower-http cors, clap v4, base64 v0.22, rand v0.9)
- Add AppState with JWT config, instance ID, and DB pool
- Rewrite error module with typed AuthError enum and GoTrue-compatible JSON responses
- Add auth/jwt.rs with HS256 JWT encode/decode and Claims struct
- Add auth/password.rs with Argon2 password hashing and verification
- Rewrite model/mod.rs with User, UserResponse, TokenResponse, Session, RefreshToken
- Add middleware/auth.rs with AuthUser and AdminUser extractors
- Implement all GoTrue-compatible HTTP endpoints:
  - POST /signup - user registration with email/password
  - POST /token?grant_type=password|refresh_token - authentication and token refresh
  - POST /logout - session revocation (local/global/others scope)
  - GET/PUT /user - get and update current user
  - POST /verify - email confirmation and password recovery verification
  - POST /recover - initiate password recovery
  - POST /resend - resend confirmation/recovery emails
  - POST /otp, /magiclink - magic link / OTP flow
  - GET/POST /admin/users - admin user management
  - GET/PUT/DELETE /admin/users/:id - admin user CRUD
  - GET /health - health check with DB ping
  - GET /settings - server capability settings
- Rewrite main.rs to use environment-based config without clap CLI
- Add CORS, tracing, and timeout middleware via tower-http
- All unit tests pass (JWT encode/decode, password hashing, error responses)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: address PR review comments for APS DSL compliance

build-skill.md:
- Fix elicit-domain SET sources to use USER_INPUT for inference
- Initialize PLANNED_FILES as [] instead of empty string
- Move RETURN after SET PLAN_APPROVED to avoid unreachable code
- Accumulate validation results in FOREACH loop, return once after

aps-v1.1.16.agent.md + VS Code template:

commit.message:
chore(docker): Refactor sandbox state management and improve Docker integration (#1068)

* Refactor sandbox state management and improve Docker integration

- Removed FileSandboxStateStore and SandboxStateStore classes for a cleaner architecture.
- Enhanced LocalContainerBackend to handle port allocation retries and introduced environment variable support for sandbox host configuration.
- Updated Paths class to include host_base_dir for Docker volume mounts and ensured proper permissions for sandbox directories.
- Modified ExtensionsConfig to improve error handling when loading configuration files and adjusted environment variable resolution.
- Updated sandbox configuration to include a replicas option for managing concurrent sandbox containers.
- Improved logging and context management in SandboxMiddleware for better sandbox lifecycle handling.
- Enhanced network port allocation logic to bind to 0.0.0.0 for compatibility with Docker.
- Updated Docker Compose files to ensure proper volume management and environment variable configuration.
- Created scripts to ensure necessary configuration files are present before starting services.
- Cleaned up unused MCP server configurations in extensions_config.example.json.

* Address Copilot review suggestions from PR #1068 (#9)

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: deer-flow
repository.description: An open-source SuperAgent harness that researches, codes, and creates. With the help of sandboxes, memories, tools, skills and subagents, it handles different levels of tasks that could take minutes to hours.

commit.message:
polish: add depth and shadow to CardTopic for a less flat UI (#262)

* Initial plan

* polish: add depth and shadow to CardTopic for a less flat UI

Co-authored-by: jason-shprintz <119976085+jason-shprintz@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: move shadow props to outer wrapper so they aren't clipped by overflow:hidden

Co-authored-by: jason-shprintz <119976085+jason-shprintz@users.noreply.github.com>

* Simplify card View and add white background

Collapse the multi-line <View> style into a single-line array for readability and add backgroundColor: '#fff' to the card style. The white background fixes rendering (e.g. shadow/overlay) and ensures consistent card appearance across platforms; no other behavior changes were made.

* Wrap Animated.View to preserve shadow

Move the shadow/container styles to a non-animated outer View and confine the scale/opacity animation to an inner Animated.View (new animatedWrapper style). Removed the shadow backgroundColor so the inner gradient is visible. This prevents the shadow/elevation from scaling with the card during the bounce animation and fixes related visual artifacts/clipping on Android.

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: jason-shprintz <119976085+jason-shprintz@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(m2): thin end-to-end slice — engine, headless mode, game loop

* feat(m2): thin end-to-end slice — engine, headless mode, game loop

Implements M2 (cryptd-7uw): the minimal slice that proves the full
L1 architecture is wired up and runnable end-to-end without LLM.

New packages:
- internal/engine: deterministic rules engine (NewGame, Move, Look)
  with typed errors NoExitError and LockedError
- internal/interpreter: RulesInterpreter maps text commands to
  EngineActions (go <dir>, look, quit and aliases)
- internal/narrator: TemplateNarrator produces fixed-template
  narrations for all event types
- internal/renderer: CLIRenderer reads stdin / writes stdout
- internal/game: Loop wires engine+interpreter+narrator+renderer;
  drives full pipeline until quit or context cancel

CLI:
- crypt headless --scenario <id>: runs headless mode; respects
  CRYPT_SCENARIO_DIR env var for scenario lookup path

Tests:
- Unit tests in all packages (no build tag)
- Integration tests in internal/game with adversarial fakes covering
  ctx cancel, channel close, quit event, narrate/render error paths
- E2E test (build tag e2e) compiles binary and runs scripted session
- Coverage ≥ 90% on all M2 packages (engine 93.8%, game 92.1%,
  narrator/renderer 100%, interpreter 95%)

Fixtures:
- testdata/scripts/minimal-run.yaml: M2 acceptance script

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(m2): address review — state corruption, quit narration, context, types

Addresses all issues surfaced by Cursor Bugbot review:

- engine: validate destination room before mutating GameState so a
  failed Move never leaves state corrupted (state-before-error-check)
- engine: hidden connections now return NoExitError (undiscoverable
  until revealed) instead of LockedError
- engine/doc.go: remove premature 'combat, inventory' claims
- game/loop: route renderer-emitted quit events through dispatch so
  quit narration ('Farewell, adventurer.') is rendered before exit
- game/loop: render quit narration after dispatch returns quit event
- game/loop: map LockedError -> 'locked_door' event, NoExitError ->
  'no_exit' event so narrators can give specific feedback
- narrator: add 'locked_door' and 'no_exit' template narrations
- renderer/cli: move stdin scan into goroutine (startOnce) so
  Render never blocks on input and ctx cancellation is honoured
- cmd/crypt: use filepath.Join for cross-platform path assembly
- cmd/crypt: rename usage strings from 'crypt' to 'cryptd'
- e2e: build binary as 'cryptd' matching documented binary name
- testdata/scripts: fix header comment (no --script flag exists)
- tests: remove unused errRenderer; add locked_door/no_exit/unknown
  dispatch coverage; use select+timeout in renderer tests

Coverage: engine 91.2%, game 97.6%, narrator/renderer 100% (total 91.6%)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(engine): hidden connections not leaked in exitList

exitList filtered over all room.Connections without checking type,
causing hidden connections to appear in LookResult.Exits and
MoveResult.Exits — contradicting the engine's own policy that hidden
connections are undiscoverable until revealed.

Now exitList skips entries where conn.Type == "hidden", consistent
with Move which already returns NoExitError for hidden directions.

Added tests:
- TestLook_HiddenConnectionsNotInExits: verifies north (hidden) absent
- TestMove_HiddenIsNoExit: verifies hidden direction returns NoExitError
Updated minimal.yaml: added secret_room with hidden north exit from entrance.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(game): propagate internal engine errors from dispatch instead of swallowing them as no_exit

NoExitError and LockedError are expected game conditions mapped to distinct
events. Any other error from Engine.Move indicates state/scenario corruption
and must propagate so callers can surface a diagnostic.

Added TestLoop_MoveInternalErrorPropagates to cover the new branch.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(m2): address review round 4 — unknown conn types, scanner errors, naming

- engine: reject unknown connection types in Move (only open/stairway
  traversable; locked/hidden already handled); adds TestMove_UnknownConnectionType
- game: handle renderer 'error' events as fatal loop errors; adds
  TestLoop_RendererErrorEventPropagates
- renderer: surface bufio.Scanner.Err() as error event after scan loop;
  remove inaccurate ctx comment
- e2e: rename TestHeadless_MinimalRunScript → TestHeadless_MinimalRunCommands;
  fix error message 'crypt headless' → 'cryptd headless'
- testdata/scripts/minimal-run.yaml: align schema with testutil.Script
  (name/steps/expect instead of scenario/expect_output)
- CHANGELOG.md: correct binary name crypt → cryptd
- beads: update cryptd-7uw description to match shipped interface

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(renderer): propagate write errors from Render; add write-error test

fmt.Fprintf/Fprintln/Fprint errors are now returned so the game loop
can abort on a broken output pipe. Added TestCLIRenderer_RenderReturnsWriteError.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Initial plan

* fix(engine): inject clock for determinism; PlayMode set by composition layer

- Engine.Now is an injectable func() time.Time (default time.Now) so
  log timestamps are deterministic in tests and replays
- Remove PlayMode from engine.NewGame — it is a UI concern set by
  cmd/crypt after NewGame (state.PlayMode = "headless")
- Add TestMove_UsesInjectedClock to verify deterministic timestamps
- CHANGELOG: crypt validate → cryptd validate

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(renderer): wire ctx into scanner goroutine; fix bead description

Co-authored-by: jmf-pobox <155204947+jmf-pobox@users.noreply.github.com>

* bd sync: 2026-03-10 17:10:57

* docs(copilot): update instructions — PR review workflow, fix binary names

- Add PR Review Workflow section documenting correct use of
  copilot-pull-request-reviewer as reviewer at PR creation
- Explicitly prohibit @copilot comments (triggers coding agent / sub-PRs)
- Add Feature Branch Workflow section (moved from old Workflow section)
- Update project state: M2 in progress on feat/m2-thin-e2e
- Fix all stale 'crypt' → 'cryptd' binary name references

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(m2): address review round 7 — path traversal, scanner goroutine, unknown_action, settings

- cmd/crypt/main.go: reject scenario IDs containing path separators or '..'
  to prevent path traversal (copilot review)
- internal/renderer/cli.go: accept io.ReadCloser; close on ctx cancellation
  so Scan() actually unblocks — fixes misleading 'goroutine leak' comment
- internal/renderer/cli.go: fix Render comment ('room description' → 'room ID')
- internal/narrator/template.go: add explicit 'unknown_action' case
- .claude/settings.local.json: remove from tracking; add to .gitignore
- CLAUDE.md, docs/distribution.md: add files from merged sub-PR content

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(engine): sort exits for deterministic order

Map iteration over room.Connections is non-deterministic. Sort the
exits slice before returning so MoveResult.Exits and LookResult.Exits
are stable across calls, tests, and replays.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(m2): address review round 8 — loop cancel, path traversal, comments

- internal/game/loop.go: wrap ctx with WithCancel/defer cancel so
  scanner goroutine is cancelled when loop exits (quit or error)
- cmd/crypt/main.go: robust path traversal check — reject volume names
  and verify resolved abs path stays within scenarioDir prefix
- e2e/headless_test.go: fix inaccurate 'Build stdin from script steps' comment
- CHANGELOG.md: fix 'room description' → 'room ID header'
- CLAUDE.md: fix 'crypt headless/validate' → 'cryptd headless/validate' (x2)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(m2): address review round 9 — path check, scanner, staticcheck, docs

- cmd/crypt/main.go: use filepath.Rel for path containment check to
  avoid root-dir edge case with string prefix approach
- internal/renderer/cli.go: remove io.Closer auto-detection to avoid
  closing os.Stdin on ctx cancel; callers own reader lifetime
- internal/renderer/cli_test.go: update cancel test to write before cancel
  so goroutine exits via ctx.Done on send
- CLAUDE.md, .github/copilot-instructions.md: add staticcheck to quality gates
- docs/distribution.md: replace go install with go build -o cryptd

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(engine): include destination room in LockedError message

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(m2): rename 'go install' section to 'Build from source'; close M2 bead

- docs/distribution.md: rename section and table entry from 'go install'
  to 'Build from source'; note that go install produces a 'crypt' binary,
  not 'cryptd', so go build -o cryptd is the correct approach
- .beads/issues.jsonl: close cryptd-7uw (M2 epic complete)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
